verses.text: 'Make haste, O God, to deliver me,' David cries, asking that those who seek his life be shamed and turned back. His need is urgent. There are times the soul can only plead for God to come quickly.
'Make haste, O God, to deliver me,' David cries, asking that those who seek his life be shamed and turned back. His need is urgent. There are times the soul can only plead for God to come quickly.
Let those who mock with 'Aha, aha' be turned back for their shame. He commits his scorners to God. The taunts of the wicked will recoil upon themselves.
Let all who seek God rejoice and magnify Him; yet, 'I am poor and needy: make haste unto me, O God.' He ends owning his need. The lowly heart casts its whole urgency on the God who is its only help.
